subject: science

primary.justification: The chapter's entire domain is one-dimensional motion — measuring time and distance, calculating speed, distinguishing uniform from non-uniform motion, and representing motion through distance-time graphs. C-2.1 is a verbatim match: it covers describing one-dimensional motion (uniform, non-uniform) using physical measurements (position, speed, and changes in speed) through mathematical and diagrammatic representations. The chapter's activities, exercises, and diagram tasks all operate within this definition.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to describe and compare uniform and non-uniform motion by measuring time and distance, calculating speed, and representing motion through distance-time graphs.
